fields.title: Memory format helpers
fields.description:
Create extensions/tau/src/memory/format.ts with pure helpers:

- ENTRY_DELIMITER constant = '\n§\n'
- parseEntries(raw: string): string[] — split file content into entries, trim, filter empty
- joinEntries(entries: string[]): string — join entries with delimiter
- charCount(entries: string[]): number — total char count including delimiters
- renderPromptBlock(bucket: 'memory' | 'user', entries: string[], charLimit: number): string — render the system prompt block with header showing usage %

Header format:
══════════════════════════════════════════════
MEMORY (your personal notes) [67% — 1,474/2,200 chars]
══════════════════════════════════════════════
entry1
§
entry2

For 'user' bucket, header says 'USER PROFILE (who the user is)'.
Return empty string when entries is empty.

Also create test/memory-format.test.ts with comprehensive tests for all helpers.

Reference: .reference/hermes-agent/tools/memory_tool.py (MemoryStore._render_block, _read_file, _write_file methods)
fields.notes: Implemented extensions/tau/src/memory/format.ts and extensions/tau/test/memory-format.test.ts. Added delimiter parsing/joining, char counting, prompt block rendering, and comprehensive tests. npm run gate passes in extensions/tau/.
